nativeRange.habitat: Reef slopes, lagoons, and intertidal to subtidal zones in shallow tropical and subtropical waters; seldom found deeper than 15 m

decorPreferences: stable flat or angled rockwork surface

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae; supplemental spot feeding with meaty foods such as mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, or prepared LPS coral foods has been reported to improve growth and coloration. Feeding tentacles are generally extended only at night. Weekly target feeding is commonly recommended.

goodWith: non-aggressive LPS corals with adequate spacing
avoidWith: corals placed within sweeper tentacle reach (5–10 cm)
highly aggressive LPS corals (e.g., Galaxea, Euphyllia without spacing)

summary: Goniastrea is an LPS brain coral (family Merulinidae) known in the trade as War Coral and Honeycomb Brain Coral. Generally considered beginner-friendly; tolerates moderate light and flow with weekly spot feeding.
careNotes:
TAXONOMIC NOTE: Goniastrea (Milne-Edwards & Haime, 1848) is a genus of roughly nine recognized species within the family Merulinidae (Order Scleractinia). The genus was historically placed in Faviidae, but was reclassified following molecular studies. In the aquarium trade it is most commonly sold simply as 'Goniastrea sp.' without species-level designation. Distinguishing features include shared corallite walls (meandroid to cerioid patterning), which separate it from the closely related Favia/Dipsastraea genus, where corallites do not share walls. The common trade names 'War Coral' and 'Honeycomb Brain Coral' refer to this genus, though these names are occasionally used loosely for other brain-patterned LPS genera.

CARE OVERVIEW: Goniastrea is generally considered one of the more forgiving LPS corals for reef aquariums. Its tolerance of a range of lighting and flow conditions, combined with relatively undemanding feeding requirements, makes it broadly accessible. Care level is commonly rated as beginner-to-intermediate across sources; one source (ReefChasers) rated it 'MODERATE' due to its aggression management requirements, while the consensus across the hobby suggests it is manageable for beginners with proper coral spacing.

LIGHTING: Moderate light intensity is generally advised, roughly 75–200 PAR. ReefChasers notes an upper range of 125–250 PAR; at this intensity, acclimation from lower PAR (75–100) over several weeks is advisable to prevent bleaching. Excessively high light has been reported to cause tissue stress and color loss. LED, T-5, and metal halide lighting have all been reported to work well at appropriate intensities.

FLOW: Moderate water movement is generally recommended — sufficient to keep detritus off coral tissue and deliver food particles without stressing polyps. Direct strong laminar flow should be avoided as it can impede feeding and stress the tissue.

PLACEMENT: Typically placed on stable rockwork in the middle of the aquarium. The encrusting to dome-shaped colony form settles well on horizontal or gently angled surfaces. Adequate spacing from neighbors is critical: sweeper tentacles have been reported to extend 5–10 cm (approximately 3–4 inches) beyond the colony margin, primarily at night, and can sting neighboring corals. Plan spacing accordingly and monitor for expansion over time.

AGGRESSION: Semi-aggressive (occasionally rated aggressive in some sources). Sweeper tentacle extension at night is well-documented; tentacles have been reported to reach approximately 5–10 cm. ReefChasers classifies aggression as 'AGGRESSIVE' primarily due to these sweeper tentacles. Careful spacing from all neighbor corals is strongly advised.

WATER CHEMISTRY: Standard reef chemistry applies. Calcium, alkalinity, and magnesium dosing is generally advised for sustained skeletal growth. Goniastrea has been reported to tolerate moderate nutrient levels (nitrate 10–25 ppm, phosphate 0.05–0.2 ppm per ReefChasers) without significant color loss, which is more permissive than many SPS corals.

FEEDING: Primarily photosynthetic. Supplemental spot feeding once weekly has been reported to noticeably improve growth rate and color. Small meaty foods such as mysis shrimp, frozen brine shrimp, zooplankton, or prepared LPS coral foods are all accepted when offered during evening hours when feeding tentacles are extended.

COLONY SIZE: Wild colonies of Goniastrea retiformis have been reported to reach up to 50 cm in diameter and up to 1 m height. In captivity, growth is slow and colonies generally remain smaller; purchased frags (typically 3/4–1 inch) can grow steadily into fist-sized colonies over years under good conditions.

PARAMETER NOTES: Temperature range is averaged from ReefChasers (77–84°F / 25–29°C) and standard reef recommendations (24–27°C) — final range 25–28°C adopted. Salinity: standard reef SG 1.025–1.026 applied (ReefChasers did not specify a salinity range). PAR range 75–200 adopted per task research consensus; ReefChasers states 125–250 PAR, which represents a somewhat higher lower bound. Both ranges are plausible; moderate acclimation is advised for new specimens.
breedingNotes: Fragmentation is the standard captive propagation method — Goniastrea colonies can be fragged with bone cutters or a bandsaw; frags readily encrust and grow on frag plugs or rubble. Sexual reproduction in the wild occurs via broadcast spawning; G. favulus has been documented as a simultaneous hermaphrodite that can self-fertilize (unusual among broadcast spawners). Deliberate captive sexual breeding is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: ReefChasers – Goniastrea Coral Care Guide
primary.notes: Primary source for water parameters, PAR, flow, aggression, and care level. LiveAquaria (listed primary for coral-saltwater slice) consistently returned homepage redirects rather than species-specific pages and could not be used as primary. ReefChasers used as primary per fallback rule.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Goniastrea retiformis
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy (Merulinidae / Scleractinia), native range confirmation, colony size (up to 50 cm diameter, up to 1 m height), and habitat (reef slopes, shallow waters). Not used for husbandry parameters.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Goniastrea favulus
additional.notes: Cross-checked native range (East Africa, Madagascar, Maldives, SE Asia, Japan, Pacific islands) and depth range (seldom deeper than 15 m). Also noted simultaneous hermaphrodite reproduction mode. Not used for husbandry parameters.

additional.name: World Wide Corals – Goniastrea search
additional.notes: Confirmed trade category as LPS and scientific name 'Goniastrea sp.' from product listings. No detailed care parameters available on product pages; general care guides page did not include Goniastrea-specific guide.
